same cost and are available for use until exhausted. This service level has the highest quality of service (QOS1), and is designed for groups which require smaller and irregular amounts of computer time.

When SL2 users exhaust their credits they can continue to submit jobs at Service Level 3 until more credits are purchased.

For current costs and details of how to purchase, internal University
of Cambridge users please refer to the [charges page](https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/charges).

## Service Level 3 – Free Usage[¶](#service-level-3-free-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 3 (SL3) operates with the medium quality of service, QOS2. QOS2 is lower than QOS1 which is used in SL1 & SL2. This service level is designed for groups with medium usage requirements who currently do not have funding to pay for their usage, thus an immediate conversion of funds into credits is not required.

SL3 is capped with a maximum number of usage credits per PI per quarter. This has been introduced to promote a more even usage of the free time on the system. Currently each PI may receive **200,000 CPU core hours** and **3000 GPU hours** per quarter.

There is no guaranteed minimum usage level for SL3 and there is no concept of expiry or of moving core hours across quarters.

Once a group in SL3 has consumed all its allowed core hours in a quarter, jobs can still be submitted at Service Level 4.

This coordinator of the HALOS-SL2-GPU might use this to add 10000 GPU hours to the HALOS-SL2-SPQR1-GPU subproject assigned to the user spqr1. Compute hours may be added to a project by a designated project coordinator user. Reducing the compute hours available to a project is also possible by adding a negative number of hours via the –time= syntax, e.g. the following command undoes the above:

gdeposit --time=-10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu

same cost and are available for use until exhausted. This service level has the highest quality of service (QOS1), and is designed for groups which require smaller and irregular amounts of computer time.

When SL2 users exhaust their credits they can continue to submit jobs at Service Level 3 until more credits are purchased.

For current costs and details of how to purchase, internal University
of Cambridge users please refer to the [charges page](https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/charges).

## Service Level 3 – Free Usage[¶](#service-level-3-free-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 3 (SL3) operates with the medium quality of service, QOS2. QOS2 is lower than QOS1 which is used in SL1 & SL2. This service level is designed for groups with medium usage requirements who currently do not have funding to pay for their usage, thus an immediate conversion of funds into credits is not required.

SL3 is capped with a maximum number of usage credits per PI per quarter. This has been introduced to promote a more even usage of the free time on the system. Currently each PI may receive **200,000 CPU core hours** and **3000 GPU hours** per quarter.

There is no guaranteed minimum usage level for SL3 and there is no concept of expiry or of moving core hours across quarters.

Once a group in SL3 has consumed all its allowed core hours in a quarter, jobs can still be submitted at Service Level 4.

## Service Level 4 – Residual Usage[¶](#service-level-4-residual-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 4 (SL4) operates with the lowest quality of service, QOS3. QOS3 is the

This lists the charge for each job in the CompHrs column. Since this example queries usage of a -CPU project, these are CPU core hours. Similarly, for a -GPU project they would be GPU hours.

**I would like to add core hours to a particular member of my group**

```
gdeposit -z 10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

This coordinator of the HALOS-SL2-GPU might use this to add 10000 GPU hours to the HALOS-SL2-SPQR1-GPU subproject assigned to the user spqr1. Note that if a compute hour limit applies to the parent of the project in the project hierarchy - i.e. if the parent project HALOS-SL2-GPU has an overall compute hour limit (which it almost certainly does) - then the global limit will still apply across all per-user projects.

Compute hours may be added to a project by a designated project coordinator user. Reducing the compute hours available to a project is also possible by adding a negative number of hours via the –time= syntax, e.g. the following command undoes the above:

```
gdeposit --time=-10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Renewal / Procurement Correction
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user wants to extend an existing storage project but believes they may have accidentally requested a new storage purchase instead. The safe handling is to stop them from activating anything new until support has verified which service record should be extended and whether the mistaken request can be corrected or needs to be cancelled or reissued.

ACTION
1) Ask the user not to activate the mistaken newly purchased storage project.
2) Confirm which existing project is the one that should be extended.
3) Check internally whether the mistaken request can be removed or converted.
4) If cancellation or reissue is required, direct the user to the appropriate purchasing or finance route.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hi Matt,

Please leave the accidentally purchased storage project unallocated while we verify whether the mistaken request can be corrected or whether it needs to be cancelled and reissued.

Once we confirm the correct path, we will tell you whether any purchasing or finance action is needed on your side.

Best wishes,
HPC Support
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: Yes (Self Service) [1][2]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has raised a purchase order for a new Research Data Store (RDS) but intends to extend the existing storage instead of provisioning a new project [1]. The existing storage project is "rds-mk652-kstarkstar" with a project ID of "bUoO2gzww9Q" [1]. The user wants to extend the license for another year with the same permissions and 5TB capacity [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Delete the new RDS storage request as it was submitted in error [7].
2. Instruct the user to upload the purchase order against the storage extension for the particular storage they wish to extend [7].
3. Verify that the license for the current storage is extended correctly and matches the user's requirements [1][7].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm the exact purchase order number and the date it was submitted?
- Are there any specific requirements or constraints for the storage extension?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We will delete the new RDS storage request and instruct you to upload the purchase order against the storage extension for your existing project "rds-mk652-kstarkstar". Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The user should be aware that the School of Biological Sciences 'SBS' resource allocation scheme ended on 31 Dec 2022, and any storage projects not yet renewed have been flagged as ‘suspended’ in the storage portal [2].
- The user should also be aware that the license for the current storage will be extended to match the user's requirements, and the storage capacity will be increased to 5TB [1][7].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-20650
[2] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/sbs.html::chunk::0000
[7] : HPCSSUP-78876

# End of SBS allocation scheme[¶](#end-of-sbs-allocation-scheme "Permalink to this headline")

Important

The School of Biological Sciences ‘SBS’ resource allocation scheme ended on 31 Dec 2022.

Any PIs and users still wishing to retain their current storage projects should respond to the automated renewal notices sent from the self-service portal prior to 31 Dec. Or alternatively, contact the servicedesk at support@hpc.cam.ac.uk for assistance in identifying the relevant projects or migrating the data elsewhere.

## Key Deadlines[¶](#key-deadlines "Permalink to this headline")

### From 1 January 2023[¶](#from-1-january-2023 "Permalink to this headline")

* Any storage projects not yet renewed have been flagged as ‘suspended’ in the
  storage portal.
* Data owners and managers can still generate quotes and submit purchase orders to renew,
  which will be automatically backdated to be effective from 1 Jan.
* Access to data is still possible during the 30 day grace period for late renewals,
  after which access to the data will be lost.

### From 30 January 2023 (end of grace period)[¶](#from-30-january-2023-end-of-grace-period "Permalink to this headline")

* User access to unrenewed projects will be lost.
* All data in projects will **only be accessible on request**.
  Access will be provided in a read-only mode in cases where it is necessary to migrate
  data to an alternative location on CSD3 or offsite.

### From 1 July 2023[¶](#from-1-july-2023 "Permalink to this headline")

* In accordance with our [terms and

headline")

Warning

The WBIC platform contained a number of different storage areas for
users, all of which will be **retired** with the closure of the platform.

All data should be migrated to an alternative storage platform on
CSD3 or downloaded to another location before the platform is
shut-down.

Below we outline alternative services on CSD3 for each storage area that
are available for purchase so that you can replicate the WBIC
environment as best as possible.

#### /lustre[¶](#lustre "Permalink to this headline")

The WBIC dedicated Lustre filesystem (/lustre) is replaced by our [RDS
storage service](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/research-data-store)
which provides equivalent large-scale scratch storage.

All CSD3 users get a free 1TB personal scratch space, but all
group-shared capacity must be purchased through our [Storage self-service
gateway](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/RDS/)
Owners of group directories will be contacted separately to make arrangements.

#### /home[¶](#home "Permalink to this headline")

All WBIC users had 100GB of /home space, however on CSD3 this is reduced
to 40GB. Users with usage greater than 40GB will be required to either
reduce their usage under this amount, or to purchase additional RFS or
RDS storage and move excess data to these areas.

#### /data[¶](#data "Permalink to this headline")

WBIC users also had access to the /data storage location, which benefited
from the same resilient storage platform as /home, including hourly
snapshots and data replication, designed for datasets requiring durable
backups.

A similar

# End of SBS allocation scheme[¶](#end-of-sbs-allocation-scheme "Permalink to this headline")

Important

The School of Biological Sciences ‘SBS’ resource allocation scheme ended on 31 Dec 2022.

Any PIs and users still wishing to retain their current storage projects should respond to the automated renewal notices sent from the self-service portal prior to 31 Dec. Or alternatively, contact the servicedesk at support@hpc.cam.ac.uk for assistance in identifying the relevant projects or migrating the data elsewhere.

## Key Deadlines[¶](#key-deadlines "Permalink to this headline")

### From 1 January 2023[¶](#from-1-january-2023 "Permalink to this headline")

* Any storage projects not yet renewed have been flagged as ‘suspended’ in the
  storage portal.
* Data owners and managers can still generate quotes and submit purchase orders to renew,
  which will be automatically backdated to be effective from 1 Jan.
* Access to data is still possible during the 30 day grace period for late renewals,
  after which access to the data will be lost.

### From 30 January 2023 (end of grace period)[¶](#from-30-january-2023-end-of-grace-period "Permalink to this headline")

* User access to unrenewed projects will be lost.
* All data in projects will **only be accessible on request**.
  Access will be provided in a read-only mode in cases where it is necessary to migrate
  data to an alternative location on CSD3 or offsite.

### From 1 July 2023[¶](#from-1-july-2023 "Permalink to this headline")

* In accordance with our [terms and
